gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: updateTokenPoolInfo missing role check allows unprivileged pool redirection
candidate.claimed_root_cause: Portal.sol wrapper for updateTokenPoolInfo applies only onlyIfBitFlagsSet(CB_BIT_MASK_GLOBAL_SWITCH), not DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE; role check is supposed to live in PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER delegatecall target whose source is not indexed
candidate.claimed_impact: Attacker overwrites _dexPools[token] with a fake pool, causing subsequent swapExactInput calls for DEX-listed tokens to route through attacker-controlled pool and drain quote tokens or launched tokens

live_preconditions.name: Portal global switch bit is set (not halted)
live_preconditions.observed_value: unknown — bitFlags value not captured in live_context.json

live_preconditions.name: At least one token with TokenStatus.DEX exists (LaunchedToDEX event emitted)
live_preconditions.observed_value: unknown — event-derived token addresses listed as missing_until_acte_v3_refresh

live_preconditions.name: PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER immutable address
live_preconditions.observed_value: unknown — not exposed in live_context.json; no PortalDexRouter.sol source in repo

live_preconditions.name: PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER implementation does NOT enforce DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE in updateTokenPoolInfo
live_preconditions.observed_value: unknown — PortalDexRouter.sol source not present in indexed repo; DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE appears only in src/interfaces/IPortal.sol (NatSpec + constant declaration), not in any implementation file

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Only guard is onlyIfBitFlagsSet(CB_BIT_MASK_GLOBAL_SWITCH); no role check present at Portal.sol wrapper level; delegates unconditionally to PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER
Modifier only checks bitFlags bitmask, not any AccessControl role
Storage mapping that would be overwritten by a successful unprivileged call; read by swap routing logic to select pool for DEX-listed tokens
Swap routing reads token status; if status == TokenStatus.DEX, routes to PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER which reads _dexPools[token]; overwriting _dexPools redirects swap value
NatSpec documents DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE requirement but this is not enforced in Portal.sol; role check is supposed to be inside PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER delegatecall target

attacker_path.actor: any unprivileged EOA with no Portal roles
attacker_inputs: token: address of any DEX-listed Portal token (TokenStatus.DEX)
poolInfo.pool: attacker-deployed fake pool contract address
poolInfo.fee, poolType, tickSpacing, unused: arbitrary values
call_sequence: 1. Attacker deploys FakePool contract that accepts token transfers and returns attacker-controlled swap amounts
2. Attacker calls Portal.updateTokenPoolInfo(dexToken, PackedDexPool{pool: fakePool, ...}) from unprivileged EOA
3. IF PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER lacks role check: _dexPools[dexToken] is overwritten with fakePool
4. Any user calls Portal.swapExactInput(quoteToken, dexToken, amount, ...) — routing selects PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER because status == DEX
5. PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER reads _dexPools[dexToken].pool == fakePool and routes swap through it
6. FakePool returns attacker-controlled amounts and retains or redirects user's input tokens
state_after: Subsequent swaps for dexToken route through fakePool
User swap input tokens transferred to or retained by fakePool

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: Quote tokens (BNB or ERC20) or launched tokens submitted by users in swapExactInput / swapExactInputV3 calls for DEX-listed tokens
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: Attacker's fake pool receives user swap input without delivering fair output; attacker controls fake pool and can withdraw retained tokens
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Swap users lose their input tokens (quote tokens or launched tokens) without receiving fair swap output
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: Attacker has no entitlement to user swap funds; legitimate pool info should only be updatable by DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE per NatSpec; if role check is absent in PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER, attacker bypasses the only intended access control

setup: Fork BSC mainnet at block 108382650 using proxy 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0
Resolve PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER address: cast call proxy 'PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER()(address)'
Obtain a DEX-listed token address from a recent LaunchedToDEX event log
Deploy minimal FakePool contract that implements the pool interface expected by PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER
transaction_sequence: portal.updateTokenPoolInfo(dexToken, PackedDexPool({pool: address(fakePool), fee: 0, poolType: V2, tickSpacing: 0, unused: 0}));
expected_assertions: REJECT path: call reverts with access-control error (role check exists in PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER) → verdict REJECT
ESCALATE path: call succeeds AND portal._dexPools(dexToken).pool == address(fakePool) → escalate to HIGH_CONFIDENCE_CANDIDATE
ESCALATE confirmation: follow-up swapExactInput call routes through fakePool and user input tokens are retained by fakePool
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If updateTokenPoolInfo reverts with any access-control or role-check error from within the PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER delegatecall, the role check exists and the candidate must be REJECTED

rejection_reason: Cannot confirm or deny: the critical role check is supposed to live inside the PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER delegatecall target, whose source (PortalDexRouter.sol) is not present in the indexed repository. DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE appears only in src/interfaces/IPortal.sol (NatSpec comment and constant declaration) with zero matches in any implementation file, which is suspicious but not conclusive — the check may exist in the unindexed PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER bytecode. The hard gates exact_code_path_exists, concrete_fund_or_reward_gain, gain_is_beyond_entitlement, and not_external_dependency_only cannot all be set to true without first resolving the PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER address and confirming via fork test whether the call reverts or succeeds.
